page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 4 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv9 arm64 architecture, utilizing the Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data shows average performance across most standard compute and memory operations. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests fall within the middle 50% tier, as do the Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression represents a relative strength, placing in the top 25% tier with a rate of 14.4 GB/sec. Conversely, Passmark CPU scores, including extended instructions, are positioned in the bottom 25% tier, while the Passmark CPU encryption test registers in the bottom 10% tier, indicating a potential bottleneck for cryptographic workloads despite hardware AES support.

This instance is designed for compute-bound workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. It is suited for tasks such as static web serving, where it achieves an average throughput of 10.11 GB/sec, and Redis database operations, processing 9.23 million SET operations per second. However, its limited 8.0 GB memory capacity and weak performance in complex instruction sets and LLM inference make it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale machine learning tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture provides a balanced compute-to-memory profile for lightweight, parallelizable microservices.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this arm64 instance includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and offers an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across standard compute, memory bandwidth, and web serving tasks, with a notable strength in single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, it exhibits weaker performance in Passmark CPU tests and cryptographic operations. The c8gb.xlarge is suited for lightweight compute-bound workloads, static web serving, and Redis operations that benefit from dedicated physical cores, offering a cost-efficient ARM-based resource profile for parallelizable microservices.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated ARMv9 Graviton physical cores, 8.0 GB of memory, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for lightweight microservices and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 8.0 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.
tagline: A compute-optimized ARMv9 instance with dedicated physical cores and balanced memory bandwidth for lightweight, parallelizable microservices and web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz.
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance.
Exhibits lower relative performance in cryptographic and extended instruction benchmarks.
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware.
Best suited for static web serving, Redis operations, and lightweight compute-bound tasks.
